name: 2D Airfoil (NACA, simpleFoam)
description: 2D airfoil at 8 deg angle of attack using Spalart-Allmaras turbulence model. Pre-meshed C-grid domain with ~10k cells. A classic validation case for lift and drag prediction.
learning_objectives: Run a 2D steady-state RANS airfoil simulation
Observe pressure distribution and lift generation on an airfoil

mesh.title: Prepare Mesh
mesh.description: This case ships with a pre-built C-grid mesh (~10k cells). No meshing commands needed — the mesh is ready to use.
mesh.tip: The mesh is a structured C-grid wrapping around the airfoil. Check the boundary file to see the 4 patches: inlet, outlet, walls (airfoil surface), and frontAndBack (empty for 2D).

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure freestream velocity at 26 m/s with 8 deg angle of attack. The inlet and outlet use freestream-type BCs, the airfoil surface (walls) uses no-slip.
boundaries.tip: The velocity components (25.75, 3.62, 0) give 26 m/s at 8 deg AoA. Change these to adjust speed or angle of attack. Remember to update liftDir and dragDir in forceCoeffs too.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm and Spalart-Allmaras turbulence. 500 iterations with residual-based convergence control (1e-5 threshold).
solver.tip: The case typically converges in 200-300 iterations. Watch the residuals — all should drop below 1e-5.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. This is a small 2D case and should complete quickly.
run.tip: If residuals stall, try adjusting relaxation factors in fvSolution. For this case, p=0.3 and U=0.7 work well.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize pressure coefficient on the airfoil surface. Check forceCoeffs for lift (Cl) and drag (Cd) values. Expected Cl ~ 1.0-1.2 for NACA 0012 at 8 deg AoA.
postprocess.tip: In the results view, look at the pressure field. The low-pressure region on the upper surface (suction side) is what generates lift.
